reviews.title: Poorly run & outdated hotel in a bad area
reviews.text: I usually have a policy of only writing positive reviews but I feel that this place, although not awful, will really spoil your Fiji experience. It has the potential to be good but it’s in a bad area (ladies of the night on the streets outside), there’s nothing around it, the service at the hotel is poor, the rooms are dated, maid service is awful (rooms weren’t done until 5pm or later every day and something was always missed - usually no towels left), WiFi is appalling (it doesn’t reach the rooms and in the communal areas it’s as good as my dial up internet was in 2002). The staff although pleasant enough are somewhat indifferent to guests and lacking direction regarding customer service. The whole place needs a revamp, some staff training and a decent internet connection. They should also get off on the right foot by turning up to collect you at the airport as agreed and not leaving you stranded for 30 minutes and left to make your own way there. My advice...stay somewhere else.
reviews.response:

With no hotel guide in the room this was a hard one to work out. After turning the knob no air conditioning happening and obviously no control as to how hot or cold you got. In the middle of the night I managed to open the very heavy sticking slider balcony door to let cooler air in.The hope was then the mosquitoes wouldn't attack.Standing on the balcony was unnerving in that it had a serious slope towards the pool. The hand rail was swinging free as the bottom stay for it was rusted away. The bed was Ok apart from the bad taste colors draping over the end of the bed. Would be good to add some Fiji art to this lack luster space.

I paid for our dinner in cash and one of the staff accused me of not paying which she later apologised for. However, the welcome did not feel very warm and the room/bathrooms are very tired and worn out.

reviews.title: Acceptable

reviews.title: Good 3 star hotel
reviews.text: Good location near shops,nicely laid out hotel with good breakfast and attractive pool area. Good room but bathroom badly in need of renovation. Only one gripe.--It dismays me that so much wealth from tourists pours into Fiji yet the local people struggle.  I asked for a taxi from the hotel to the airport., primarily to support the locals. They are a dime a dozen and need our business. The Mercure provided me with their hotel transfer at over twice the price which I declined as this money only goes back into the conglomerate coffers. Support the friendly local cab drivers and specify you want a taxi. You'll pay 60 % less and support individuals and their families

reviews.positives: The surrounding was very convenient. The WiFi was good. Breakfast was also good!
reviews.negatives:

The air conditioning was either very strong or off. I couldn’t control it. I slept with strong air conditioning and caught cold next morning. I can’t believe they changed over $400 per night with this poor condition. The bathroom was really dirty. I could see a lot of white spots on the shower glass wall. The shower floor was very dirty with reddish mold between the tiles. They should put more effort to clean it throughly.

reviews.title: Meh...
reviews.text: The Mercure is certainly the scrappier little brother of the Novotel. It advertises a gym, but the gym is at the Novotel. The breakfast buffet is overpriced and disappointing. The rooms are average, to say the least, with no power point in the bathroom. The staff are ok - friendly enough, but at breakfast I was given the option of putting the cost on to my bill, which I chose. Five minutes later someone came over and told me I needed to pay in cash then. I like Accor, but I'll stay elsewhere the next time I'm in Nadi. There's nothing horrific about the Mercure - there's just nothing that would bring me back.

Comfy beds, clean pool, playground could use some updating (one of the swings was half broken), TV was very fuzzy.  Staff at dinner were helpful. Went to use the bathroom in the middle of the night and found 8-10 small cockroaches climbing all over the sink and counter top. The next morning on check out the staff member was not very engaging, no enquiry to how our stay was etc. Once I had paid and returned room keys I advised about cockroaches in case he wanted to arrange some pest control. I received a blank look, and he said ok. Then I said ok… and he kept looking at me so I just said thank you and left. An “sorry about that, we’ll arrange someone to look into it” would have gone a long way into turning me in to a repeat client. As it is, I probably wouldn’t stay again.

Friendly, helpful staff, decent food. One dinner was especially nice. 

Nice to have free airport shuttle. Hotel based taxis cost twice as much as flagging one down on the road. 

This is not a great resort area hotel. They have made a little enclave on the main road in a semi-commercial area. No beach within easy walking distance. Though a travel service can book you for day trips to outer islands for a price.

reviews.title: Quite big but old hotel
reviews.text:
We spend 4 nights in the hotel as part of our Fijian holiday. Hotel is spacious with lot of opportunity to expand. Parking is free and secure. Large garden and a decently big outdoor pool. But rooms are quite old with many malfunctions. Flush in our room was not working properly.

Hardly any change in buffet breakfast, so if you are staying here for more than two days, you will get bored.

Food at the hotel restaurant is decent.

reviews.title: Mercure Nadi Hotel
reviews.text: Don't let the "set within five acres of beautiful tropical gardens" fool you.  This is fronted on a highway in a business district.  The staff is very cordial and professional.  My room was nice, but the air-conditioning was marginal.  Although the restaurant is promoted in their ads, when I was there (two evenings) my only dinner options were a modest buffet or pizza.  The food was good, but not outstanding.  I stayed one night and held a day room as my flight did not leave until 10:00 pm.  As it is indeed convenient to the airport, I would recommend this as a transit hotel, but not as a honeymoon or vacation accomodation.
reviews.positives: Convenient to airport.
reviews.negatives: Air conditioning not adequate.  Restricted menu choices - Buffet or pizza!
reviews.response:

reviews.title: 5 nights in Nadi
reviews.text: Usual the hospitality of the Fijian people is fantastic, the location is within walking distance to various food and one Supermarket (Jetpoint). Breakfast was ok to fill my stomach in the morning, love the fruits (so sweet and juicy).  overall i think feels like an old hotel. The comment I have concerns the room that i stayed in. The standard areas are clean, floor was swept but not mopped. Underneath the bed was not clean for a while looking at the think layer of dust. The aircon only has 2 settings, On and Off, no temperature control at all. The toilet had an unusual design where the lowest point of the shower area is lower than the drainage, therefore standing water will remain in the shower. The surfaces in my toilet was always covered in a fine white dust, i suspect it comes of the plaster on the ceiling. the balcony was a nice touch to enjoy the pool view but the chairs had bird droppings and was not clean when i arrived. Perhaps hard to prevent due to the abundant wildlife around the hotel.  thank you but i will not stay here again.

reviews.text:
The hotel is in a fantastic location, perfect for enjoying the nightlife, dining, and a nearby supermarket. You’ll find enough bars, restaurants, and shops within walking distance to make it a very convenient stay.

While the hotel is clean, it’s a bit outdated and could benefit from some updates. The staff are friendly and helpful, though there’s no fitness centre. For the price, you're mostly paying for the prime location rather than the quality of the accommodations.

Overall, if you're here to enjoy the local scene, this hotel is a solid choice, but it could use a bit of work to match the price tag.

reviews.title: My Stay at Hotel Mercure Nadi
reviews.text: I stayed in Hotel Mercure Nadi for two nights. The transfer coach arrived very late that I had to wait at the parking lot for one hour. However, when I arrived at the hotel, everything was smooth and easy. The reception clerk was very attentive and I was able to be in my room very quickly. The room is clean and very comfy. I had a good rest.
reviews.positives: Near the airport, very friendly staff, quick service and clean rooms
reviews.negatives: when it rains, some of the alleys get wet and slippery
reviews.response: Thank you for your review Elrico!

reviews.title: Glad it was only one night.  The price was way to high for the facility.
reviews.positives: The breakfast buffet had a great variety of options and was included. Great Indian restaurant across the street.
reviews.negatives: The pictures of the hotel are deceiving.  On the surface the hotel looks like it would be OK but the facility is old and they have done the bare minimum to update it to cover up the issues. The room smelled of mold.  The bathroom tile was grouped over and painted to cover the mold.  The spring mattress wasn't comfortable and you could feel the springs.  The aircon smelled and only worked on high so you either froze or had no aircon.

reviews.title: Mercure Nadi Airport Hotel
reviews.text: Nice hotel for the price.  Disappointed that the exterior door of my room can be seen from the outside.  I booked the room through Agoda the day before and they took 1/2 hour of research to find my reservation.  Took another 1/2 hour to find the hotel shuttle bus driver at the airport. He parked his bus at the parking lot and I had to find him. The taxi drivers who sits outside the hotel charge ridiculous prices.  He discouraged me from riding the city bus.  He said it was unsafe.  Wanted to charge me FJ$70 for a tour of Denauro and Nadi town, and wanted to bring his wife.  I ended up spending FJ$2.40 on buses.  No guided tour needed.  You can walk through the whole town within 15 minutes.
reviews.positives: 2 beds - one Queen, one single
reviews.negatives: room exterior door accessible from the outside.  there is no elevator/lift.  the toilet does not flush well.  The shower does not have a door, only a glass wall separating it from the sink and toilet.  no bathtub.  The taxi drivers outside were very pushy.

Because of the poor accommodation we chose to go to the airport several hours before required.  Very disappointing as this was our last night in Fiji having returned from 6 nights on a beautiful island.  Terrible last impression of Fiji sadly.

reviews.title: good
reviews.text:
The Mercure Hotel felt a little older than it appeared in the photos on the website, but overall it was clean and well maintained. However, in the room we did see cockroaches and there were quite a lot of mosquitoes, with noticeable gaps around the door.

We stayed for two nights, and the breakfast menu was quite similar each day. That said, the freshly made omelets were excellent and very satisfying. The coffee was served piping hot, and there was also a wide variety of bread to choose from.

The staff’s service was truly wonderful. They were very kind in explaining how to take the bus, how to get to the bus stop, the taxi fares, and the way to the airport.

There was also a small convenience store just across the road where we could buy water and snacks, which made the location very convenient.

Overall, I would recommend staying here.

reviews.title: ⸻  ⭐️⭐️⭐️☆☆ (3/5) – Convenient stay with friendly service
reviews.text:
I stayed at Mercure Nadi and had a generally pleasant experience, especially for a short stopover.

The biggest advantage of this hotel is its location — it’s very close to Nadi Airport, which makes it super convenient for early flights or late arrivals. The staff were incredibly friendly and welcoming, which really stood out and made the stay more enjoyable.

The rooms were clean but quite basic and slightly dated. They could definitely benefit from some refurbishment to bring them up to a more modern standard. Comfort-wise, the essentials were there, but don’t expect anything luxurious.

The pool area was nice and relaxing, and the overall atmosphere of the hotel felt laid-back and tropical. Breakfast was decent with a reasonable variety, though not particularly standout.

Overall, Mercure Nadi is a solid choice for convenience and friendly service, but it’s more of a budget-friendly, practical stay rather than a high-end resort experience.

reviews.text:
We recently stayed at Mercure Nadi for 2 nights and found the location to be one of its biggest strengths. It’s incredibly convenient — just a short walk to a supermarket, several restaurants, a money exchange, a bottle shop, and plenty of nightclubs. Having everything so close made getting around super easy.  

The standard triple rooms definitely feel like they’re due for an update, but for our needs, they did the job. They were safe, secure, and clean — exactly what we needed as a base while exploring Nadi.

Overall, a solid stay in a fantastic location, with room for improvement in the accommodation itself.

reviews.title: Great Stay
reviews.text:
We arrived late due to a delayed flight but check in was smooth. We were assigned a room in the third floor. We were warned by receptionist that there were no lifts in the hotel and that was okay since we only had one luggage (but really it would have been nice to have someone assisted us with it)
Buffet breakfast is truly just basic and almost same everyday. The last breakfast we had, they ran out of pancakes and tropical juice which was never refilled. It's still okay for me though. 
The swimming pool was AMAZING! My son loved it. It never gets crowded so we had plenty of time to enjoy it. There were also swings, a slide  (which i think would really need safety checking) and hammocks (love these!)
The room gets cleaned and arranged everday. Towels are changed. Would have loved to have at least one rug in the room though. There was also leaking in the ceiling of our bathroom but it wasn't bothering us so I did not have to report it. Nice verandah where we had our meals. 
Prices in the restaurant are reasonable i guess.
Staff were polite. Had very minimal interactions with them but they seem okay. 
Thanks for welcoming us! 😊
reviews.response:
Dear Judy,
Thank you for taking the time to share your detailed feedback following your recent stay with us. We are pleased to hear that your check-in process was smooth despite the delay in your arrival, and that you and your family were able to enjoy our swimming pool and outdoor facilities, especially the hammocks and slides.
We truly appreciate your kind words about the cleanliness of the room and are glad it met your expectations. It is always encouraging to hear that families, particularly children, have had a memorable and enjoyable time at our property.
That said, we are equally grateful for your constructive comments regarding areas where we can improve. We apologize for the inconvenience caused by the lack of elevator access to your room and will look into ways to better support our guests with luggage assistance in the future. Your comments regarding the breakfast variety and the unavailability of certain items such as pancakes and tropical juice on your last day are noted.

reviews.title: Exceptional
reviews.text:
We stayed at Mercure Nadi again for two nights toward the end of our trip, returning to Nadi earlier than planned and choosing the hotel because of its great location.  This time we booked the deluxe triple rooms, which are definitely a step up from the standard triples — fresher and nicer overall — though similar in size and layout.  Still, they were comfortable and convenient for what we needed, and the location remains a huge plus. 

It’s only about $5 by taxi to Nadi Town, $12 to the airport and there are plenty of shops, restaurants, and other essentials within easy walking distance.  TIP: walk to Jetpoint to catch a taxi with a "taxi" sign on top and a meter inside, else you 100% be overcharged - sometimes by double the normal price.

The hotel breakfast a good selection of fresh fruit, cereals, muffins, and hot items like eggs, hash browns, and bacon.  It offered enough variety to kick-start the day without fuss.

Check‑in and check‑out were seamless, and overall we were very happy with our stay. 

We’d definitely recommend the Mercure Nadi for its convenience, comfort, and value.  The staff are lovely too.

reviews.title: Central, safe & clean
reviews.text:
I booked 2 rooms (3 adults) for an  8 day stay both rooms had a queen size and single size bed in each. 

The drive from the airport was 10-15min & cost approx. $25FJD via a local taxi (not the yellow cabs) - super fast & cheap. 

Check-in was friendly & efficient. We were allocated rooms on the 2nd floor  next to each other but not connected.

Please note there are 3 levels & if you require mobility or accessibility friendly rooms, request ground floor as there aren’t any ramps or elevators. 

Our rooms were simple & clean (and wonderfully air conditioned). This is an older hotel so you will find a fair amount of wear & tear but all in all the rooms were comfortable & spacious with the required amenities for a comfy stay.

 We had balconies overlooking the pool and family areas which were somewhat private even though they faced the balconies of block of rooms located across from us. 

The bar and dining areas are either undercover or located on the deck with a view of the pools. 

The staff is wonderful - they’re so warm & hospitable! We loved every interaction we had with them & they were nothing but personable & lovely our whole 8 day stay. 

The cocktails are approx $25FJD ($22 during happy hour). They’re generous serves & a large variety listed on the drinks menu. We tried a few (most actually lol) and enjoyed them. If you’re not a cocktail person, beer/wine/spirits/soft drinks/juice are available & prices are fairly reasonable.

Our stay came with a buffet breakfast which didn’t disappoint! The omelette station was our favourite! 

The kitchen does a wonderful local specialty - Kokoda, which is raw fish with fresh veges in a coconut cream, lemon juice sauce as well as some yummy pizzas. 

The onsite Spa/Beauty services are wonderful. I did a recovery massage with reflexology for approx $130FJD which went for 90min in total. 

The location is awesome. Queens Rd has supermarkets, laundry services, bars, restaurants, BBQ stands, clubs & a movie cinema.

Denarau Island/Marina is a 20 min cab ride which costs approx $30FJD. 

We loved everything about our stay here but mostly the location and the people! 

It’s great for couples and families.

Even with its location being so central, it’s generally quiet & peaceful - but you will hear an airplane overheard sometimes due to the close proximity of the airport. 

We wil definitely come back to stay here with our extended family - we really enjoyed our time in Nadi.

reviews.title: Close to Nadi
reviews.text: A very convenient location. We took a local bus to Nadi town on our first day and explored around. Went to the produce market, then a couple of the shopping centres, the handicraft market and finally the temple before heading back. All for $2 each way for 2! The resort itself is nice but the rooms are a bit dated. It’s more of a motel than a hotel. Breakfast was plentiful and the staff are all very friendly. We stayed for 3 nights before heading up to Lautoka to stay at another resort. Would recommend for a short stay.

reviews.title: Mercure Nadi
reviews.text:
Travelled in a group of 7 - 5 adults, 2 teenagers with 3 standard rooms.  The rooms are basic, if you’re looking for somewhere as a base whilst you explore Fiji and are not expecting 5 stars in room type then I recommend giving the Mercure a go. Honestly I was surprised that being under the Mercure banner the rooms were so desperately in need of a renovation. Our bathrooms were ‘clean’ but the grout was dirty, amenities were worn and we had a water feature from the ceiling when upstairs showered. We also had a little friend who lived under the bed we named ‘Stuart little.’ Again understandable due to the terrain. 
Outside in the gardens is a different story they are beautiful and well maintained. The pool area, restaurant and bar area were awesome and we spent a lot of time out there.  
We walked the local streets and were greeted by everyone “bula, bula, bula”  we were made to feel so welcome. Breakfast was decent and staff were lovely. Be prepared to pay for taxis - it cost $60 fjd one way to Denarau.

reviews.title: A functional stay - Great Location, but perhaps not the best value for money.
reviews.positives: The hotel's location is a major plus—it's conveniently close to the airport and within walking distance of many shops and restaurants. The pool and garden areas are well-maintained, providing a lovely atmosphere. Most of the staff were polite and welcoming, with a special mention to Dhiraj for his excellent service. The breakfast was decent to start with.
reviews.negatives:
The room was very basic with minimal facilities, which didn't feel like good value for the money. The lack of a free airport shuttle was surprising given the proximity to the airport. Also, while the breakfast quality was fine, the lack of variety over our week-long stay became repetitive. A particularly frustrating experience was when the restaurant staff refused to heat a sandwich we had bought outside the hotel.
Be aware that there are no lifts; we were on the third floor and had to carry all our luggage up ourselves as reception did not offer assistance. Additionally, the trips and taxis arranged by the front desk were more expensive than those sourced externally.

reviews.title: Fantastic Post-island hotel retuning back to Airport
reviews.text:
We wanted somewhere close to Nadi International Airport after our 5nights at different islands (Plantation Resort and Mana Island). Mercure Hotel provided this safe haven for a family with 2 children to reflect, readjust prior to departure flights. 
Local Buses were $1FJD pax to local Nadi Handicraft/Nadi Fruit/Vege Market or $10/Taxi. Nearby Chinese and Japanese restaurants were also conveniently located. 

Breakfast was good enough for the kids and adults. 
Enjoyed the stay with Accor hotels.
reviews.response:
Dear Samuel,
Thank you so much for your lovely review! 
We are truly delighted to hear that Mercure Nadi provided a comfortable and convenient stopover for your family after your island adventures. It is wonderful to know that our location, close to Nadi International Airport and local amenities, added ease and value to your stay.
We are especially pleased that the breakfast, nearby dining options, and accessibility to local transport worked well for both the adults and the little ones. It is always our aim to offer a welcoming and relaxing environment where families can unwind and recharge before their journey ahead.
Thank you for choosing to stay with us and for your kind words about Accor Hotels. 
We hope to welcome you and your family back again soon, whether it is before or after your next Fijian island escape!

Kind Regards
Jack Work
General Manager
Mercure Nadi

reviews.text: Mercure is located in Nadi town and super close to both Denarau and the Nadi Airport which is so convenient for activities and getting to and from the airport. Pool is kept clean, the restaurant is always clean too and the buffet breakfast is tasty. The staff is wonderful. Value for money in price too. Ticks all the boxes. The rooms are older but they are clean and room service is top tier. I found things more impressive here than at some other 5 star properties we stayed at during this trip. There’s an ATM and dairy (convenience store) almost just outside the front door.
reviews.response:

ホテルも綺麗で雰囲気もよく良かったのですが、ナンディ事態が何もないところなので、ホテル周辺がにぎわっているという感じはありません。
空港から近く、離島に行くときのフェリーのピックアップもしてくれるホテルなので、前泊などにお勧めします。
reviews.response:
Bula Shigematsu, Thank you for taking the time to provide feed back on your recent stay. We are happy that you enjoyed your stay and the services provided. It is always a pleasure to receive positive comments. 
We would love to welcome you back at the Mercure Nadi again sometimes soon." Vinaka Vakalevu"

reviews.title: 快適なホテル
reviews.text:
今回は、出発前の1泊で利用いたしました。
翌日の朝が早かったため,朝食もとらずに出発しましたが、とても良いホテルでもう少し滞在したかったです。
室内も清潔で施設も、プール、レストラン、バー、スパなどがあり申し分ないです。
海が見えないのと、アメニティにハブラシ、スリッパがないこと、3階まで荷物を手運びしないといけないのが、難点でした。
reviews.response: Thank you for your review Kayoko!

reviews.title: グッドサービス
reviews.text: とにかく、従業員の方々が凄く親切に接してくれて、非常に快適に過ごせました。特にこれと言った問題点も無く、お勧めできるホテルだと思います。
reviews.positives: 従業員の愛想がよく、気持ちいい!!ホテルも清潔で、良いホテルです。
reviews.negatives: もう少し遅くまで、レストランやバーが開いていると良い。
reviews.response: Thank you for your review Manabu!

reviews.title: 無料の送迎
reviews.text: 空港からの無料ピックアップを利用しました。離島へ行く前の１泊だけでしたが隣にスーパーやレストラン、ベーカリーなどあり便利です。部屋でのwifiはちょっと弱い感じでした。
reviews.response:
Bula Mariko, Thank you for posting your review and I was pleased to note that you enjoyed the food in the restaurant.
We trust that we shall welcome  you again to Mercure Nadi very soon.

Regards
Anna Rabaka
Assistant Manager

reviews.title: BULA！
reviews.text: 12月16日から6泊しました。空港からも近く無料でホテルのシャトルバスも出ています。マーケット及びマリーナも近く、ベースにするなら価格・ロケーション共に良い所だと思います。
reviews.positives: 料金がリーズナブルだったので、少し心配していたのですが、エアコンも完備され、快適且つ清潔でした。
reviews.negatives: 朝食及び夕食ともローテーションのバフェットで、選択肢が無いところ。
reviews.response: Thank you for your review Ryuichi!

reviews.title: 値段相応だと思います。
reviews.text:
今回はキングベットのシングルに泊まりましたが、部屋は広くてバスタブがありました。
エレベーターがないのですが、荷物はホテルスタッフが運んでくれますので、遠慮なくお願いしました。チップは要りませんでしたよ。
リゾートホテルではないので、海は見えませんが、庭にプールがあり、楽しめます。
近くにスーパーはありますが、お惣菜のような食品は有りません。ビールやアイスクリーム、日用品はありました。
ホテルの前にコンビニもあり、便利です。
ホテルの前の道路を車に注意しながら渡るとレストランがあり、ココのピザは美味しかったです。驚くようなサイズなので、オーダーには気をつけて下さい。

reviews.title: よかったです！
reviews.text:
2泊だけ宿泊しました。
朝ごはんが宿泊に含まれてるところがよかったし、美味しく食べれました！
お部屋もタオル交換ちゃんとあり、綺麗でした！徒歩圏内でスーパーとバーガーキングあって困りません！空港に行くためのバスも止まります。一点、Transferはメモに記載する欄があったので記載して、その時点で送迎してくれるのかな？って思ったけど、心配で日本を出国する3日前にメールするも返信なく、当日のチェックイン2時間前くらいに返信がありました。。その対応の遅さだけは残念でしたが、あとは満足です！

reviews.title: 無難なホテル
reviews.text: 立地、サービス、総合したら値段相応のいいホテルだと思います！ただ、階段横、プールの近くは結構うるさいです。部屋に水はありませんので近くのスーパーで買いましょう。
reviews.response:
Dear Miyu,
Thank you for taking the time to share your feedback following your recent stay with us.
We are pleased to hear that you found our hotel to offer good value for its location and service. Your kind words are truly appreciated.
We also sincerely apologize for the inconvenience caused by the noise near the stairs and the pool area. Your comments have been noted, and we will take them into consideration as we continuously work to improve the comfort of our guests.
Regarding the in-room water, we appreciate your suggestion. While we currently do not provide bottled water in the rooms, we are exploring options to enhance our in-room amenities in the future.
Thank you again for your valuable feedback. We hope to have the opportunity to welcome you back and provide an even more enjoyable experience.

Kind regards
Jack Work
General Manager
Mercure Nadi

reviews.title: 不建议中国游客居住
reviews.text: 我们在居住美居酒店的期间，遇到几件事：1，由于道路修改，船务公司不送到美居酒店，距离酒店一公里的地点转运免费车辆。我们遇到转运过程中印度司机故意不让我们乘车，在再三确认我们是一日游客人还有酒店的住宿后，依然假装听不明白，拒绝我们上车，最后我们和另外两名中国游客走回去的。2，入住前几天美居发生中国游客在房间丢失400美金。3，酒店有免费送机的巴士，酒店房故意告知只有一班车，凌晨5点，后来我们在斐济的朋友与酒店交涉，乘坐了他们6点的免费巴士，朋友认为酒店是故意希望我们早些离开，只有2.8公里的路程，朋友说5点出发过去机场出境都没有上班。我们将遇到的事情告知朋友，朋友告诉我们，最近斐济的印度人认为我们中国人有钱，并且中国人不愿意申诉，遇到问题就主动给点钱算了，所以最近遇到很多类似的事情，他说他头两天还有两个朋友坐了船，坐船务公司的免费巴士，非让他们给了每人5元斐济币才上车，但是其实是免费的，所以我们在去美居路上的遭遇是肯定想我们多给钱。还有酒店前两天的失窃事件，也是针对中国人的，所以出门要将钱放进保险箱和随身携带。在美居酒店所遭遇的歧视待遇确实让人很不开心。所以不建议中国人居住，因为无论是到达美居酒店的路上还是在酒店容易发生让人不愉快的问题，尽量避免遇到这样的针对中国人的歧视发生。

reviews.title: Great stay at Mercure!
reviews.text: I've had a nice 3 nights' stay at Mercure. The staff were friendly and helpful. The room was comfortable too! I've also had my dinners for all 3 nights at the hotel. The food was great and not very expensive.

reviews.title: Not what we selected
reviews.text:
Was transfered to Novotel Nadi, the sister hotel. Was given wrong room which had defective TV. Took hotel management ages to sort out and finally move rooms. 2nd last night we had termites in the room. While towels were changed everyday the toilet, bathtub and shower areas were not cleaned properly. Old and unmaintained facilities. Restaurant staff are very friendly but some do get confused with your orders.
I would not go back

reviews.title: Older hotel, conveniently located for a short stopover.
reviews.positives: The Mercure is very conveniently located for a quick stopover.  The staff were excellent, the breakfast was good and we couldn't complain about the room.  This is not a resort hotel.
reviews.negatives: All things considered there is nothing negative to say.  This is an older hotel.  It has been renovated, but the room layouts are still quite old.  It is not near any attractions like beaches.  It is however good for a short stopover, if not for a holiday in Fiji.

reviews.positives: Mercure is just a short drive away from the airport and we went with a transfer booked through Booking.com to making going to and from the airport streamlined. Check-in was quick and easy the staff were friendly and food was reasonably priced at the restaurant. The room was clean and the pool was great for the hot and humid weather. It was also close to supermarkets, movies and other restaurants which served a variety of food.

reviews.title: Excellent
reviews.positives: We stayed upon arrival and departure in Nadi at the Mercure. Highly recommend it. I will go back just for the breakfast buffet. It was outstanding!!! The location was convenient to the airport (15 fijian taxi ride away) and within walking distance of restaurants and shopping. You feel miles away when inside the hotel grounds. The rooms are clean and simple. The a/c worked great. It can get busy near the pool area from the beginning of the day...we preferred staying in a room a bit away from all the excitement. Loved hanging out on the variety of shaded hammocks, cabanas, loungers.

reviews.title: Solid stop-over hotel Nadi
reviews.positives: The Mercure is a solid hotel for a stop-over in Nadi. Its clean and tidy with helpful staff and quiet rooms. The breakfast is good and  food in the restaurant is pretty good basic fare and reasonably priced. Nice pool and good happy hour for drinks The location is close to airport (15$FJD for taxi), and reasonably close to Denerau (about $20FJD taxi).
reviews.negatives: Quite expensive for what it is
